replit.md.content:
# Academy CRM System

## Overview

This is a comprehensive CRM system designed for E1 Sport Champions Academy to manage players, track activities, handle payments, and manage subscription status. The application is built as a full-stack web application with a React frontend and Express.js backend, using PostgreSQL as the database with Drizzle ORM for data management.

## Recent Changes

### August 7, 2025 - Local Database Support Added
- ✓ Created flexible database configuration supporting both Replit (Neon) and local databases
- ✓ Added support for local PostgreSQL and MySQL connections
- ✓ Created comprehensive local setup guide (LOCAL_SETUP.md)
- ✓ Added .env.example template for easy local configuration
- ✓ Created setup script for automated local database configuration
- ✓ Updated database connection logic to auto-detect environment (Replit vs local)
- ✓ Added detailed troubleshooting guide for common local setup issues

### August 4, 2025 - Receipt & Payment System Improvements  
- ✓ Fixed subscription period update in edit player - now properly updates both start and end dates
- ✓ Removed contact information from receipt footers for cleaner presentation
- ✓ Simplified payment actions by removing Details button (Receipt handles both functions)
- ✓ Enhanced monthly transaction filtering by player with date range support
- ✓ Fixed PDF generator to exclude contact details for professional receipts
- ✓ Streamlined payment UI for better user experience
- ✓ Fixed subscription period selection during player creation - now respects user-chosen dates
- ✓ Enhanced subscription period display to show actual duration instead of "Monthly Plan"
- ✓ Improved form data submission to properly handle custom subscription end dates

### July 31, 2025 - Player View Enhancement & Smart Renewal Payment System
- ✓ Fixed payment records to display player names instead of "Unknown Player"
- ✓ Added Player ID display to view player modal for easy reference
- ✓ Added medical information section in player view with conditions, allergies, and emergency contact
- ✓ Enhanced subscription renewal with custom date selection (start and end dates)
- ✓ Implemented intelligent renewal payment system that handles outstanding balances
- ✓ Added payment priority logic - old subscription balance paid first, then new subscription
- ✓ Created separate payment records for previous balance and new subscription periods
- ✓ Added visual outstanding balance warnings in renewal summary
- ✓ Enhanced renewal form with comprehensive payment and subscription tracking
- ✓ Improved user experience with clear payment allocation and balance information


### July 30, 2025 - Player-Based Attendance System Implementation & Enhanced Subscription Renewal
- ✓ Created player-focused attendance system replacing session-first approach
- ✓ Built MarkPlayerAttendanceModal for direct player attendance marking  
- ✓ Developed PlayerAttendanceSystem component with player-centric interface
- ✓ Updated backend to automatically increment sessionsAttended when marking present
- ✓ Added visual session remaining indicators with low-session warnings
- ✓ Implemented automatic session calculations and renewal alerts
- ✓ Enhanced user experience with streamlined attendance workflow
- ✓ Replaced traditional session management with intuitive player selection flow
- ✓ Improved responsive design for mobile and tablet devices
- ✓ Fixed API request parameter order for proper session creation
- ✓ Enhanced mobile-first layout with adaptive grid systems
- ✓ Added proper text scaling and button sizing for different screen sizes
- ✓ Fixed date validation errors in player update API route
- ✓ Added comprehensive subscription renewal feature with session and payment editing
- ✓ Created tabbed edit player modal with renewal and edit sections
- ✓ Implemented backend renewal endpoint that resets sessions and creates new subscription cycle
- ✓ Enhanced renewal form to allow editing of subscription fee and total sessions allowed
- ✓ Removed Activities and Reports from navigation sidebar for cleaner interface
- ✓ Added dynamic session count display in renewal summary
- ✓ Changed notification buttons from "Contact Player" to "Renew Subscription" that navigate to renewal functionality
- ✓ Made notification bell icon clickable to scroll to renewal notifications section
- ✓ Enhanced notification system to open edit player modal with renewal tab by default
- ✓ Implemented smart notification filtering to automatically remove renewed players from notifications
- ✓ Updated notification logic to only show players who actually need renewal (critical session count or expiring subscription)
- ✓ Fixed renewal workflow to automatically update subscription end dates and reset session counters

### July 30, 2025 - Installment Payment System Removal & Session Fixes
- ✓ Completely removed installment payment functionality from frontend and backend
- ✓ Deleted installment-payment-modal.tsx and installment-tracker.tsx files  
- ✓ Removed paymentInstallments table from database schema
- ✓ Fixed session creation API to properly handle date string conversion
- ✓ Fixed dialog accessibility warnings by adding DialogDescription to all modals
- ✓ Enhanced print functionality with professional styling and academy branding
- ✓ Streamlined payment system to use single payment per subscription approach
- ✓ Updated database schema and applied changes successfully

### July 30, 2025 - Enhanced Subscription Management & Payment System
- ✓ Added subscription date fields (start date, end date) to player creation and editing
- ✓ Implemented subscription pause functionality with pause reason tracking
- ✓ Created comprehensive edit player modal with subscription management
- ✓ Added additional payment modal for incremental subscription payments
- ✓ Enhanced subscription status enum to include 'paused' state
- ✓ Updated database schema with subscription end date and pause tracking fields
- ✓ Fixed payment logic to support multiple payments until full subscription amount is paid
- ✓ Added proper payment validation to prevent overpayment
- ✓ Integrated edit and additional payment functionality into players table
- ✓ Updated player creation form with subscription date selection and initial payment options
- ✓ Fixed array handling issue in player creation route for numeric fields (subscriptionFee, amountPaid)
- ✓ Implemented comprehensive renewal notification system with 7-session and 3-day triggers
- ✓ Added dynamic notification counter to dashboard bell icon
- ✓ Created renewal notifications component that displays only when notifications exist
- ✓ Enhanced payment tracking in player view modal with visual progress indicators

### July 29, 2025 - Session Cost Removal & Player Search Implementation
- ✓ Removed session cost functionality completely from database schema and frontend
- ✓ Applied schema changes to remove sessionCost column from sessions table
- ✓ Replaced dropdown player selections with searchable player input in payment forms
- ✓ Replaced dropdown player selections with searchable player input in session forms
- ✓ Created reusable PlayerSearchInput component with search, filter, and selection capabilities
- ✓ Fixed player view modal error when accessing activity properties
- ✓ Fixed session attendance tracking with correct API request format
- ✓ Updated payment API to support player-specific payment queries
- ✓ Enhanced user experience with intuitive search-based player selection
- ✓ Resolved all LSP errors and improved type safety across components

### July 28, 2025 - Database Connection Fixed & Per Session Rate Removed
- ✓ Resolved DATABASE_URL environment variable issue
- ✓ Created new PostgreSQL database instance
- ✓ Applied database schema using `npm run db:push`
- ✓ Verified application is running successfully on port 5000
- ✓ Confirmed API endpoints are working correctly
- ✓ Removed per session rate field from payment functionality and database schema
- ✓ Updated frontend forms to remove per session rate references
- ✓ Fixed LSP errors and type definitions
- ✓ Fixed renewalDate validation error by automatically calculating renewal date on server
- ✓ Verified player creation now works correctly through both API and frontend

## User Preferences

Preferred communication style: Simple, everyday language.

## System Architecture

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables for theming
- **UI Components**: Radix UI primitives with shadcn/ui component library
- **State Management**: TanStack Query (React Query) for server state management
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod validation
- **Routing**: Wouter for lightweight client-side routing
- **File Structure**: Component-based architecture with shared utilities

### Backend Architecture
- **Framework**: Express.js with TypeScript
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Neon serverless driver
- **ORM**: Drizzle ORM for type-safe database operations
- **File Uploads**: Multer for handling document uploads
- **API Design**: RESTful API with structured error handling
- **Build Process**: ESBuild for server-side bundling

## Key Components

### Database Schema
The application uses a relational database with the following main entities:

1. **Players Table**: Stores player information including personal details, activity type, and subscription status
2. **Player Documents**: Handles file uploads for player documentation (ID, medical forms)
3. **Payments**: Tracks payment records with installment support
4. **Sessions**: Records player session attendance and tracking
5. **Enums**: Predefined activity types, payment methods, and status indicators

### Core Features
1. **Player Management**: Registration, profile management, and activity assignment
2. **Payment Tracking**: Subscription fees, installment payments, and receipt generation
3. **Session Management**: Attendance tracking and subscription reminders
4. **Activity Categorization**: Support for multiple sports and activities (Karate, Football, Swimming, etc.)
5. **Document Management**: File upload and storage for player documents
6. **Dashboard Analytics**: Statistics and reporting for administrative oversight

### UI/UX Design
- **Design System**: Academy-branded color scheme with blue and red theme colors
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with adaptive layouts
- **Component Library**: Consistent UI components following shadcn/ui patterns
- **File Upload**: Drag-and-drop interface for document uploads
- **Form Validation**: Client-side validation with server-side verification

## Data Flow

1. **Player Registration**: Form submission → validation → database insertion → document upload
2. **Payment Processing**: Payment data entry → calculation of remaining balance → receipt generation
3. **Session Tracking**: Session creation → attendance recording → subscription status updates
4. **Dashboard Updates**: Real-time statistics fetching → data aggregation → visual presentation

## External Dependencies

### Production Dependencies
- **Database**: Neon PostgreSQL serverless database
- **File Storage**: Local file system with express static serving
- **PDF Generation**: jsPDF for receipt generation
- **Date Handling**: date-fns for date manipulation
- **Validation**: Zod for schema validation

### Development Tools
- **TypeScript**: Type safety across the entire application
- **Drizzle Kit**: Database migrations and schema management
- **Vite Plugins**: Development tooling and Replit integration
- **PostCSS**: CSS processing with Tailwind CSS

## Deployment Strategy

### Development Environment
- **Hot Reload**: Vite dev server with HMR for frontend
- **Server Restart**: tsx for TypeScript execution with auto-restart
- **Database Migrations**: Drizzle push command for schema updates

### Production Build
- **Frontend**: Vite build process creating optimized static assets
- **Backend**: ESBuild bundling for Node.js deployment
- **Static Assets**: Express static middleware for file serving
- **Environment Variables**: Database URL and configuration management

### File Upload Strategy
- **Storage Location**: `client/public/uploads` directory
- **File Validation**: Size limits (10MB) and type restrictions (images, PDFs)
- **Security**: File type validation and sanitized naming

The system is designed to be scalable and maintainable, with clear separation of concerns between frontend and backend, type safety throughout, and a modern development experience optimized for the Replit environment.

client/src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as CheckboxPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-checkbox"
import { Check } from "lucide-react"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Checkbox = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <CheckboxPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "peer h-4 w-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-primary ring-offset-background focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=checked]:text-primary-foreground",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator
      className={cn("flex items-center justify-center text-current")}
    >
      <Check className="h-4 w-4" />
    </CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator>
  </CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
))
Checkbox.displayName = CheckboxPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Checkbox }

client/src/components/ui/progress.tsx.content:
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import * as ProgressPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-progress"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Progress = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, value, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ProgressPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative h-4 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ProgressPrimitive.Indicator
      className="h-full w-full flex-1 bg-primary transition-all"
      style={{ transform: `translateX(-${100 - (value || 0)}%)` }}
    />
  </ProgressPrimitive.Root>
))
Progress.displayName = ProgressPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Progress }

client/src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as ScrollAreaPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const ScrollArea = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("relative overflow-hidden", className)}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport className="h-full w-full rounded-[inherit]">
      {children}
    </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport>
    <ScrollBar />
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Corner />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
))
ScrollArea.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root.displayName

const ScrollBar = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
>(({ className, orientation = "vertical", ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar
    ref={ref}
    orientation={orientation}
    className={cn(
      "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
      orientation === "vertical" &&
        "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
      orientation === "horizontal" &&
        "h-2.5 flex-col border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaThumb className="relative flex-1 rounded-full bg-border" />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
))
ScrollBar.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar.displayName

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar }

client/src/components/ui/slider.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as SliderPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-slider"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Slider = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <SliderPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative flex w-full touch-none select-none items-center",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <SliderPrimitive.Track className="relative h-2 w-full grow overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary">
      <SliderPrimitive.Range className="absolute h-full bg-primary" />
    </SliderPrimitive.Track>
    <SliderPrimitive.Thumb className="block h-5 w-5 rounded-full border-2 border-primary bg-background ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50" />
  </SliderPrimitive.Root>
))
Slider.displayName = SliderPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Slider }
